instrument.shared_text: In this experiment, you are going to predict future values of "Variable A". The experiment lasts for 40 rounds. At the beginning of the experiment, you will see data from 40 previous rounds. Then, you will have to make predictions for 40 rounds.

treatment_variations.text: In each round, you will see past values of "Variable A". Past values of "Variable A" are related to future values of "Variable A", and the relationship is stable.
In each round, you will see past values of "Variable A" and "Variable B". Past values of "Variable A" and "Variable B" are related to future values of "Variable A", and the relationship is stable.

label: Temptation: Immediacy and certainty

instrument.shared_text: Choose how you want to split your workload of 360 rows of counting (in addition to the required 10 rows per workday).

treatment_variations.text: One of today's five decisions may be randomly selected to actually split your workload. The odds of each decision being the decision-that-matters are 10%.
Explicit instrument text not in paper. Subjects see one row permanently highlighted and a reminder that only choices at this rate will be implemented.
Explicit instrument text not in paper. Subjects are informed the randomly-selected day is revealed before their day-two decisions.

simulation_flag: REAL_EFFORT: β is a structural parameter estimated from allocation choices across tasks — not directly elicitable from LLM text response

label: People Judge Discrimination Against Women More Harshly Than Discrimination Against Men

instrument.shared_text: Consider the following hypothetical scenario. A job is available in a company. A manager in the company has narrowed down the pool of applicants to the two most qualified people: one man and one woman.

treatment_variations.text: Taking into account all characteristics of the two applicants (qualifications, experience, personality, etc.), the manager knows that the woman is slightly more qualified and hiring her would bring slightly higher profits for the company. After considering everything, the manager hires the man.
The man and the woman have worked equally hard in their career. For example, both regularly studied on the weekends while their friends were out partying.

The man and the woman would suffer equally much from not getting the job. For example, both are currently unemployed, but have enough savings so that they could go without getting a paycheck for another 4 weeks. Also, both would find it equally hard to get a new job. Neither of them has to support a family.

The job is in an industry where there is no gender discrimination. A number of studies have convincingly shown that in this industry neither men nor women face discrimination in hiring decisions, nor do they face any other unfair treatment by coworkers or supervisors because of their gender.
The man and the woman would suffer equally much from not getting the job, the man and the woman have worked equally hard in their career, and the job is in an industry with no gender discrimination.

label: Behavioral Food Subsidies

instrument.shared_text: For this grocery-shopping trip, we will reimburse you for some of your groceries.

treatment_variations.text: Any item in the fruits and vegetables category will qualify for the partial reimbursement. You will be reimbursed 30% of the price on all items on your receipt in the fruits and vegetables category, up to a maximum reimbursement of $10.

You will need to choose which types of foods to be reimbursed for. There are two food categories for you to choose from: 1. Fruits and vegetables 2. Baked goods. Which category would you like to get the 30% reimbursement on?
We have 2 short surveys for you for your next grocery-shopping trip. You MUST complete this survey 4 to 48 hours BEFORE you go grocery shopping.

simulation_flag: FIELD_EXPERIMENT: outcome is actual grocery spend — not elicitable from LLM text response
